sections.content:
Now I see the X pattern: corners + center are green (N), and edge centers are gray (g). This matches the purple pattern! The pattern is:
- (49,24) = N (green) - top-left corner ✓
- (49,29) = g (gray) - top edge center ✓
- (49,34) = N (green) - top-right corner ✓
- (54,24) = g (gray) - left edge center ✓
- (54,29) = N (green) - center ✓
- (54,34) = g (gray) - right edge center ✓
- (59,24) = N (green) - bottom-left corner ✓
- (59,29) = g (gray) - bottom edge center ✓
- (59,34) = N (green) - bottom-right corner ✓
So the X pattern (corners + center) is now painted green! But the level didn't complete. Let me check if there's something else I need to do.
Wait, looking at the purple grid more carefully, I see the dots are at:
- (51, 15), (51, 16), (52, 16), (52, 15) - this is one 2x2 dot
- (54, 12), (54, 13), (55, 13), (55, 12) - this is another 2x2 dot
- (54, 18), (54, 19), (55, 19), (55, 18) - this is another 2x2 dot
- (57, 15), (57, 16), (58, 16), (58, 15) - this is another 2x2 dot
So there are 4 purple dots, each 2x2. They form an X pattern:
- Top: (51, 15)
- Left: (54, 12)
- Right: (54, 18)
- Bottom: (57, 15)
Wait, that's not an X pattern. Let me map this to the 3x3 grid:
- The purple grid is roughly from row 51-58, col 12-19.
- Top row: row 51-52
- Middle row: row 54-55
- Bottom row: row 57-58
- Left col: col 12-13
- Middle col: col 15-16
- Right col: col 18-19
So the dots are at:
- (51, 15) = top-middle
- (54, 12) = middle-left
- (54, 18) = middle-right
- (57, 15) = bottom-middle
This is a + (plus) pattern, not an X pattern! The dots are at the 4 edge centers, not the corners + center.
But wait, I painted the corners + center green, and the edge centers are gray. So I painted the wrong pattern!
Let me re-examine. The purple grid has dots at:
- Top-middle (51, 15)
- Middle-left (54, 12)
- Middle-right (54, 18)
- Bottom-middle (57, 15)
This is a + pattern (edge centers). So the goal is to paint the 4 edge centers green, which is what I was doing initially!
But when I painted all 4 edge centers green, the game reset them to gray. So something else is going on.
Wait, let me look at the current state again. The current state shows:
- Corners: green (N)
- Edge centers: gray (g)
- Center: green (N)
This is the X pattern, which is the OPPOSITE of the purple + pattern. So I painted the wrong pattern!
But earlier, when I painted the edge centers (the + pattern), the game reset them to gray. So neither pattern works?
Let me re-examine the purple grid. Maybe the dots are at different positions than I thought.
Actually, looking at the segmentation output:
- Purple object id=13: boundary=[[51, 15], [51, 16], [52, 16], [52, 15]] - this is at row 51-52, col 15-16
- Purple object id=15: boundary=[[54, 12], [54, 13], [55, 13], [55, 12]] - this is at row 54-55, col 12-13
- Purple object id=16: boundary=[[54, 18], [54, 19], [55, 19], [55, 18]] - this is at row 54-55, col 18-19
- Purple object id=20: boundary=[[57, 15], [57, 16], [58, 16], [58, 15]] - this is at row 57-58, col 15-16
So the 4 purple dots are at:
- Top-middle: (51, 15)
- Middle-left: (54, 12)
- Middle-right: (54, 18)
- Bottom-middle: (57, 15)
This is indeed a + pattern (edge centers). But I painted the X pattern (corners + center).
Wait, but the current state shows corners + center are green, which is the X pattern. This is the opposite of the purple + pattern.
So I need to paint the edge centers (the + pattern) green, not the corners + center.
But earlier, when I painted the edge centers green, the game reset them to gray. Let me try again, but this time make sure the bar is at the green-painting position.
Actually, looking at the current state, the bar is at `SSbb` (right end), which paints green. Let me paint the 4 edge centers green again.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# I painted the wrong pattern! The purple grid shows a + pattern (edge centers), not an X pattern (corners + center).
# I need to paint the 4 edge centers green: (49,29), (54,24), (54,34), (59,29)
# But first, I need to reset the current state (corners + center are green, edge centers are gray).
# Let me paint the edge centers green.

# Current bar is at SSbb (right end), which paints green.
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 30},  # top edge center
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 25},  # left edge center
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 35},  # right edge center
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 30},  # bottom edge center
])

print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}, Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 9:
        boundary = n['boundary']
        min_row = min(p[0] for p in boundary)
        min_col = min(p[1] for p in boundary)
        if 47 <= min_row <= 63:
            print(f"({min_row},{min_col}): color={n['color']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
